column1: Certainly! Below is the updated timeline with all date ranges converted into numeric years (BCE/CE format):
Timeline of Solar Deities, Messiah Figures, and Covenant Gods: Evolution and Spread

column8: Proto-solar worship, chariot imagery, foundational for later solar deities
Mitra as upholder of contracts, often with Varuna; solar and legal aspects
Sacred intoxicant, personified as a deity, linked to ritual renewal
Sun as divine judge, chariot imagery
Atenism: early monotheism, solar disk worship

column4: Lord of the Covenant, protector of agreements, local god of Shechem
column5: Hebrew: בַּעַל בְּרִית ("Baal of the Covenant")
column7: Canaanite and early Israelite religion, especially in Shechem
column8: Temple at Shechem; associated with covenants, possibly linked to local treaties and alliances; later polemicized in biblical texts

column7: Zoroastrianism spread across Persia and Central Asia
column8: Emphasized Ahura Mazda as supreme god of light; Mithra as divine judge

column4: Covenant, light, justice, sun, contracts, afterlife
column8: Yazata (angelic being), mediator, judge of souls, upholder of order

column4: Sun god, initiation cult, cosmic power, tauroctony
column8: Mystery cult, communal meals, Sunday worship, December 25th festival, solar monotheism

column6: Pure Land Sutras, statuary, temple art
column8: Central to Pure Land Buddhism; promises rebirth in the Western Paradise; emphasis on light

column4: Future Buddha, messianic figure, enlightenment, compassion
column8: Expected to appear in the future to renew the Dharma; savior figure; parallels to Mithra

column4: Prophet, dualism, light vs. darkness, savior
column8: Syncretic: draws on Mithraic, Christian, Buddhist ideas; Mithra as savior guiding souls to light

column4: Unconquered Sun, state cult, December 25 festival
column8: Festival date and iconography absorbed into Christian tradition as Christmas

column4: Light of the world, cosmic ruler, resurrection, Sunday worship
column8: Christ depicted with solar attributes, such as a radiant nimbus or chariot, echoing Sol Invictus and Mithras
Some traditions merge or parallel Maitreya and Mithra as savior figures
Examples: Baha'u'llah (Baha'i), New Age figures, continued solar motifs
